incorrects.description: Student set Nonfiction bar to wrong height on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Look at the data table. What number does Nonfiction show?

incorrects.description: Student set Nonfiction bar to wrong height on second attempt

params.description: The 60 line on the axis highlights.

steps.text: Nonfiction has 60 books. Find the 60 line on the axis. Count by 10s if you need to: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60. Set the bar to end at that line.

incorrects.description: Student set Nonfiction bar to wrong height three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System demonstrates setting Nonfiction bar to height 60, extending from 0 to the 60 line.

steps.text: Let me show you. The data table says Nonfiction has 60 books. I find the 60 line on the axis. Then I set the bar to end right there. The bar extends from 0 all the way to 60. That's how we show 60 books on a bar graph with scale of 10.
